mechanism_narrative: A low or falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces the discount rate on equities and supports risk-taking, so periods with sustained easing should precede positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered easing windows do not beat the unconditional 20d return rate, or if positive 20d returns are not more frequent when FEDFUNDS is falling over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward returns are positive in most episodes where UNRATE rises by 0.3pp or more over 3 months, or if the triggered sample has no worse hit rate than the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates, it signals stronger nominal consumer spending and better top-line revenue for large-cap firms, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60d returns are not more frequent when RSAFS is above its 6-month moving average, or if rank correlation is near zero over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable inflation signals improving real activity without immediate policy tightening pressure, which should support equity multiples and prices over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 20d returns do not concentrate in periods where INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation stays below its prior 12-month median, or if the joint signal adds no lift over INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in HOUST and PAYEMS signals housing-led employment momentum, which expands household demand and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered months with rising HOUST and PAYEMS do not show higher 20d returns than either series alone, or if the interaction coefficient is unstable across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in CPIAUCSL above recent inflation norms pressures real rates and raises the odds of policy tightening, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS stays below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if the negative 60d return association disappears when inflation is high but FEDFUNDS remains low, or if higher inflation does not precede lower equity returns in out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS trend during an otherwise expanding economy signals weakening labor income and lower consumption, so the next week’s equity returns should be negative when payroll momentum turns down sharply.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by at least 50k over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if 5d forward returns are not negative more often after payroll declines of 50k or more, or if the effect vanishes after controlling for the prior month’s market trend.

mechanism_narrative: If GDPC1 growth is weak while RSAFS remains firm, the market receives conflicting demand signals and should reprice lower over the next quarter because output is not matching spending strength.
failure_condition: Reject if 60d returns are not lower when GDPC1 growth is below trend while RSAFS stays above trend, or if the combined signal fails to outperform either proxy independently.
